title: The Unmeasured Deep

features.name: luminous underbark
features.desc-on-touch: light lives inside the wood here; it has learned to glow without burning, to measure nothing at all

features.name: the shadow's own light
features.desc-on-touch: shadow and brightness have learned to overlap here; you cannot measure where one ends and the other begins

features.name: light-route through leaves
features.desc-on-touch: shafts fall and shift — the archive holds the motion, not the moment; watching them measure nothing at all

features.name: root architecture
features.desc-on-touch: the deep roots have grown systems for sensing light they cannot see; you follow the eucalyptus's blind seeing downward

features.name: the silent fern
features.desc-on-touch: it has never needed sunlight measured; it knows light as a kind of patience, and waits in frequencies that instruments cannot record

inscription: light that escapes the measure learns to exist in many frequencies at once; the trees read it as a colour with no name

file.name: Spectral_Analysis_Void.raw
file.note: instrumentation failed; readings show more light than atmosphere should contain, all at wavelengths that refract uncertainty

exits.label: back to the chamber where light still obeys its boundaries
toward the drowned forest, where trees remember being swallowed by water and light at once
downward to the root-deep where no light travels, only understanding
